1: direct Praise the LORD! Sing to the LORD a new song, his praise in the assembly of the godly! A new song in the assembly — Revelation 5:9: they sang a new song, saying, Worthy are you to take the scroll and to open its seals, for you were slain, and by your blood you ransomed people for God from every tribe and language and people and nation. The new song of Ps 149 is the song of the Lamb; the assembly of the godly is the congregation gathered around the risen Christ.
2: shadow Let Israel be glad in his Maker; let the children of Zion rejoice in their King! Rejoicing in the King — Revelation 19:7: Let us rejoice and exult and give him the glory, for the marriage of the Lamb has come. Philippians 4:4: Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, rejoice. The King in whom Zion rejoices is the messianic King, Christ enthroned at the Father's right hand (Psalms 110:1; Acts 2:34-36).
3: shadow Let them praise his name with dancing, making melody to him with tambourine and lyre! Praise with dancing and music — the full-bodied worship that Christ restores. Luke 15:25: the elder son hears music and dancing at the prodigal's return — the type of eschatological celebration. David danced before the LORD (2 Samuel 6:14); the church worships with the fullness of human expression before the one who is David's greater Son.
4: shadow For the LORD takes pleasure in his people; he adorns the humble with salvation. God adorning the humble with salvation — 1 Peter 5:5-6: God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble. Humble yourselves, therefore, under the mighty hand of God so that at the proper time he may exalt you. The salvation that adorns the humble is the salvation Christ gives: Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest... for I am gentle and lowly in heart (Matthew 11:28-29).
5: shadow Let the godly exult in glory; let them sing for joy on their beds. Singing for joy on their beds — the joy that transcends circumstance. Acts 16:25: Paul and Silas singing hymns to God at midnight in prison, lying on their prison beds. Philippians 4:4-7: joy and peace that surpass understanding, guarding the heart in Christ. The joy of Psalms 149:5 is the joy no one can take away (John 16:22).
6: shadow Let the high praises of God be in their throats and two-edged swords in their hands, High praises and two-edged swords — the word of God as the weapon and the worship. Hebrews 4:12: the word of God is living and active, sharper than any two-edged sword. Revelation 1:16: from his mouth came a sharp two-edged sword — Christ himself is the word that cuts. Ephesians 6:17: the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God. Praise and the word are the weapons of the church.
7: shadow to execute vengeance on the nations and punishments on the peoples, Vengeance on the nations — the final judgment reserved for Christ. Romans 12:19: Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord. Revelation 19:11-16: the rider on the white horse judges with justice and executes the vengeance of God on the nations. The saints of Ps 149 participate in this judgment: Revelation 20:4: they came to life and reigned with Christ for a thousand years.
8: shadow to bind their kings with chains and their nobles with fetters of iron, Binding kings with chains — Revelation 20:1-3: the angel seizing the dragon and bound him for a thousand years. Christ's victory at the cross is the binding of the strong man (Matthew 12:29): how can someone enter a strong man's house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man? The chains of Psalms 149:8 are the chains of the cross-achieved victory.
9: shadow to execute on them the judgment written — this is honor for all his godly ones. Praise the LORD! The judgment written executed — Revelation 20:11-15: the dead judged by what was written in the books. The honor given to the godly who execute judgment is the honor of reigning with Christ: Revelation 5:10: you have made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they shall reign on the earth. The closing Praise the LORD! is the church's response to its dignified role in the final vindication.
